recommendations: add a minimal smoke test suite for generated MCP endpoints (at least import + one valid call per endpoint) and wire it into CI
pin and constrain MCP runtime dependencies (fastmcp/pydantic plus PlasmaPy-compatible ranges) to avoid resolver drift
validate endpoint naming/casing consistency (e.g.
`Alfven_speed` vs snake_case conventions) and add backward-compatible aliases
add structured input validation schemas for Astropy Quantity-bearing functions (units
equivalencies
defaults
and clear error messages)
prioritize exposing stateless formulary/atomic functions first and gate complex class-based endpoints (`MHDWave`
grid classes) behind an “experimental” flag
add timeout/guardrails for computationally heavy calls (e.g.
`spectral_density`) with configurable limits
generate richer endpoint docs in `README_MCP.md` including parameter units
examples
and expected return shapes/types
add contract tests comparing MCP endpoint outputs to direct PlasmaPy calls for numeric parity within tolerances
introduce caching for immutable dataset-backed lookups (atomic/isotope metadata) to reduce repeated overhead
add observability hooks (structured logs
per-endpoint latency/error counters) for runtime diagnostics
include a compatibility matrix (Python/PlasmaPy versions) and automate it via CI jobs
harden fallback behavior from zip-import analysis by recording confidence and emitting warnings when introspection is partial
create a small set of end-to-end client examples (CLI and programmatic MCP client) to validate real usage
add security checks for exposed endpoints (input size limits
deny dangerous object construction paths
sanitize exceptions)
split generated plugin code into stable hand-maintained wrappers vs regenerated stubs to reduce overwrite risk during re-generation

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Low intrusiveness risk and high import feasibility (0.91) supported adapter_mode=import generation.
Repository has a modern pyproject.toml, enabling cleaner dependency/introspection paths.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and plugin-level tests passed.
No runtime errors or warnings were reported during workflow execution.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing external documentation/context enrichment.
Original project test result is marked failed/empty despite reported 100% coverage metadata, indicating test instrumentation inconsistency rather than validated upstream breakage.
Quality telemetry gaps: node-level timings, LOC/size, and performance metrics are zero/unknown, limiting confidence in operational profiling.

node_performance.download_time: Node completed successfully, but per-node duration not captured; repository ingestion used zip fallback over ~286 files.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with medium complexity classification and high import confidence; DeepWiki sub-step failed but fallback LLM direct analysis succeeded.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; MCP scaffold and service files were created with import-based adapter and 22 endpoints exposed.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health/run checks passed; original project tests were not meaningfully executed/recorded (0s), so validation depth is limited.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown due to missing metrics (reported 0 MB); cannot assess empirically.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown due to missing metrics (reported 0%); likely under-instrumented rather than zero usage.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated artifacts exist, but reported generated size is 0; disk accounting/instrumentation appears incomplete.
